qualifying_org_definition: Any provider of unpaid work that can verify hours; 10 CCR 2506-1-4.311.A.1.a.iii states 'Unpaid work verified by the provider of the unpaid work' satisfies the ABAWD requirement with no restriction on organization type and no county-approved-site registry. Workfare at county-designated sites (4.311.A.4) is a separate, optional lane.
verification_instrument: ABAWD Volunteer Verification Form (state-issued, hosted on cdhs.colorado.gov/snap/abawd; submitted once per certification period; can be uploaded via Colorado PEAK portal or MyCOBenefits app, or returned to county office in person or by mail). Regulation (4.311 preamble) states client statement is acceptable verification unless questionable — the form is the standard vehicle for that statement, countersigned by the provider.
hours_formula_detail: 10 CCR 2506-1-4.311.B.1 explicitly states '20 hours per week or averaged monthly for a total of 80 hours a month'. Colorado Workfare (4.311.A.4) is a separate parallel lane that uses the allotment-over-minimum-wage formula, but the general volunteer/unpaid-work lane uses the standard federal 80-hour monthly threshold.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Neither the regulation (10 CCR 2506-1-4.311) nor the ABAWD Volunteer Verification Form imposes any restriction on the location or registration status of the verifying organization. The form asks only for 'Organization or Agency Name (if applicable)', phone, and a signature — no EIN, no in-state address, no Colorado charitable-registration requirement. Federal default (7 CFR 273.24) permits any qualifying nonprofit; absent an explicit state restriction, out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be accepted operationally.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. FNA ABAWD waivers page lists only a FY 2025 waiver for Colorado (approved 08/15/2024); no FY 2026 waiver is on file. OBBBA tightened waiver criteria statewide and Colorado did not obtain a new FY 2026 waiver.
submission_deadline: Once per certification period; no specific monthly deadline. The form is submitted when the recipient needs to document compliance, typically at recertification or when compliance is questioned.
notes:
Colorado has a clearly distinct general volunteer lane: recipients submit the state's ABAWD Volunteer Verification Form (once per certification period, signed by the nonprofit/provider) with no mandatory county pre-assignment or EDP. The state regulation (10 CCR 2506-1-4.311) explicitly accepts client statement as sufficient verification unless questioned. Workfare (county-designated sites, hours = benefit ÷ minimum wage) is a separate optional lane, not a required precursor. Confidence is medium rather than high because the training desk-aid PDFs returned 403 errors and the actual form PDF could not be retrieved to confirm its exact fields; the Hunger Free Colorado page and LII regulation text are the primary bases.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted adversarial review attempting to find a hidden prestep, EDP requirement, or approved-site registry that would refute the Tier 1 classification. Searched for: work registration/intake requirements, employment development plan mandates, approved volunteer site lists, Employment First enrollment as a prerequisite, and county-level gating. Retrieved the full 10 CCR 2506-1 regulations PDF (1.9 MB) from the Colorado SOS and extracted sections 4.310 and 4.311 via pdfminer. Key verbatim regulatory findings: (1) 4.311.A.1.a.iii — 'Unpaid work verified by the provider of the unpaid work' directly satisfies the 20 hrs/week ABAWD requirement; no restriction on organization type, no county-approved-site list, no MOU or registration required for the provider. (2) 4.311 preamble — 'client statement is considered acceptable verification unless questionable. If verification is requested from the client, case documentation must thoroughly explain why the original client statement was considered questionable.' This confirms self-reported hours with the provider verification form as the standard instrument. (3) 4.311.A.4 — Colorado Workfare is listed as a separate, parallel option, not a prerequisite. (4) Section 4.310 general work requirements impose work registration (signing the application) at intake, but this is universal SNAP registration, not a gate on the ABAWD volunteer lane. No EDP or Employment First enrollment is required before volunteer hours count — Employment First participation is an alternative compliance path, not a mandatory prestep. (5) No approved site list or provider registry found in regulation, state CDHS page, Hunger Free Colorado, or any county page. Multiple search angles (EDP requirement, case manager mandate, volunteer site registry, PEAK self-report) all returned no evidence of a prestep. Remote/online volunteer stance remains unknown — not addressed in regulation or public-facing guidance. Tier 1 classification is confirmed. Confidence raised to high based on direct cite to the governing regulation (4.311) and its unambiguous text permitting any provider-verified unpaid work without prestep.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula: confirmed federal_80 — 10 CCR 2506-1-4.311.B.1 explicitly says '80 hours a month'; Workfare's allotment-over-minwage formula applies only to that separate optional lane. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: silent — neither the regulation nor the form imposes any in-state or registration requirement on verifying organizations; form only requires org name, phone, and signature. (3) Geographic waivers: none statewide — FNA waivers page shows only a FY 2025 CO waiver (08/15/2024); no FY 2026 entry; OBBBA eliminated the broad high-unemployment waiver authority. (4) Submission UX: form submitted once per certification period via PEAK (online portal), MyCOBenefits app (mobile upload), in-person county office, or mail; reporting cadence is at_recertification with no monthly deadline. (5) Form fields: extracted directly from the actual PDF (Google Drive link 1Zos7-h0EkU-4L-oJ1bFKKCHTz9m98S2Z) — one-page form collects CBMS case number, volunteer name, volunteer start date, yes/no compliance checkbox (20 hrs/week or 80 hrs/month), volunteer position/nature of work, supervisor/contact name, org/agency name (optional), phone, email, signature, date. No EIN, no notarization, no hours-by-day breakdown.
